task_completion.visual_evidence: The GCompris main menu or application window is visible on the screen.
The Hangman game interface is visible, showing the gallows, letter choices, and blank spaces for the word.

task_completion.description: Progress is made in the Hangman game
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show letters being selected, appearing in the word blanks, or parts of the hangman character being drawn.

task_completion.description: A full round of Hangman is completed
task_completion.visual_evidence: The game shows a completion state, such as a fully revealed word with a success indicator, or a fully drawn hangman indicating a loss.

task_completion.description: A screenshot of the completed round is saved to the correct path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot tool (like GNOME Screenshot, ImageMagick, or a terminal command) is visible capturing the screen and saving it to '/home/ga/hangman_result.png'.

task_completion.description: The application remains stable without crashing
task_completion.visual_evidence: GCompris remains open and visible in the final screenshots; there are no crash dialogs or sudden disappearances of the app.

task_completion.description: The sequence of actions shows a logical progression
task_completion.visual_evidence: The trajectory shows a clear sequence: opening the app, navigating to the game, playing it to completion, and finally taking a screenshot.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual GCompris Hangman game
integrity.visual_evidence: Mouse clicks or interactions are visible on the GCompris interface, changing the game state over multiple frames, rather than just displaying a static image.

integrity.description: Agent used the GUI to play the game
integrity.visual_evidence: The gameplay progresses through GUI interactions (selecting letters on the screen) rather than attempting to bypass the game via terminal commands.

integrity.description: The screenshot is genuinely taken from the system
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshot action captures the actual desktop environment with GCompris running, not a downloaded or pre-existing image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: CRITICAL: The task description incorrectly states Hangman is in the 'Reading' category. It is actually located in the 'Games' category of GCompris. Do not penalize the agent if it searches in 'Reading' first, as long as it eventually finds and plays Hangman. The final screenshot must be saved to /home/ga/hangman_result.png. The scoring breakdown is: app_running (10), activity_open (20), gameplay_progress (20), round_complete (20), screenshot_saved/file_valid (15), no_crash (5), workflow_logical (10).
